title: Turn Claude Co-work Into a Self-Directing Daily Engine
description: Set up a one-phrase trigger system where Claude interviews you, chooses tasks, and autonomously executes your daily work.

cards.title: Welcome to your AI daily engine
cards.body: Transform Claude Co-work from a tool you micromanage into a self-directing system that handles 80% of your daily work. You'll learn to set up a workspace where one phrase triggers Claude to interview you and autonomously execute tasks.

cards.title: Flip the AI relationship
cards.body: Instead of you constantly prompting Claude with specific tasks, this system reverses the relationship. Claude becomes the interviewer who asks you questions, then decides what it can tackle.

analogy.text: Think of it like having a personal assistant who comes to your desk each morning, asks about your priorities, then goes off to handle everything they can while you focus on what only you can do.

cards.safe: You stay in control — Claude only works on what you approve during the morning interview.

cards.title: Create your workspace structure
cards.body: Set up three folders that will organize all your Claude interactions. This creates a reliable system where inputs and outputs have designated places.
bullets: Create a main project folder (name it anything like 'Claude-Work' or 'AI-Assistant')
Inside it, create three subfolders: context, inbox, and outbox
Context = background info Claude reads, Inbox = tasks to process, Outbox = completed work

source.author: Alex Finn
source.description: Setting up the folder structure for the Claude workspace

cards.title: Write your rules file
cards.body: Create a claude.md file in your context folder. This becomes Claude's 'brain' — it reads this first in every session to understand who you are and how you work.
cards.code:
# Claude Rules

## My Profile
- Role: [Your job title/focus]
- Current projects: [List 2-3 main projects]
- Working style: [How you prefer communication]

## Preferences
- Priority: [What matters most in your work]
- Format: [How you like deliverables structured]
- Boundaries: [What Claude should/shouldn't do]
cards.codeCaption: Customize this template with your actual details — the more specific, the better Claude can tailor its work.

cards.title: Quick check
cards.question: What's the key advantage of the rules file approach?

options.text: Claude reads your context first and personalizes its work automatically
It prevents Claude from making mistakes
It saves money on API costs

cards.correctFeedback: Exactly! The rules file means Claude understands your context from the start of every session, no repeated explanations needed.
cards.wrongFeedback: The main benefit is that Claude can proactively tailor its work to your specific situation and preferences.

cards.title: Trigger the daily interview
cards.body: Open Claude Co-work and use the magic phrase that starts everything. Claude will ask you three key questions to understand your day.
cards.codeCaption: Type this exact phrase to begin the interview process
bullets: Claude will ask: What's the #1 thing you need to focus on?
Then: What's on your to-do list today?
Finally: Any urgent items that need immediate attention?

source.author: Alex Finn
source.description: Demonstrating the daily trigger phrase and interview process

cards.title: Claude chooses and executes
cards.body: After your interview answers, Claude analyzes what it can help with and autonomously decides which tasks to tackle. No more decision fatigue about what to ask AI to do.
bullets: Claude evaluates your priorities against its capabilities
It identifies tasks that can be parallelized or automated
Work begins immediately without additional prompting from you
cards.safe: Claude will tell you what it's working on before it starts, so you can approve or redirect if needed.

cards.title: System is running!
cards.body: You've built the infrastructure and triggered your first daily workflow. Claude is now working autonomously based on your priorities. Next, we'll explore the advanced features.

cards.title: Parallel processing with sub-agents
cards.body: Claude Co-work can spawn multiple sub-agents to work on different tasks simultaneously. This is like having a team of specialists working in parallel.

analogy.text: Imagine a factory assembly line where different stations work on different parts at the same time, then everything comes together at the end.

bullets: One sub-agent might research while another writes
Multiple deliverables appear in your outbox simultaneously
Tasks that normally take hours can complete in minutes

source.author: Alex Finn
source.description: Demonstrating how Claude spawns sub-agents for parallel work

cards.title: Check your outbox for deliverables
cards.body: While Claude works, check your outbox folder periodically. Completed drafts, research files, and other deliverables will appear here automatically.
bullets: Files are saved with descriptive names and timestamps
Each deliverable includes context about what it addresses
You can provide feedback by moving items back to inbox with notes
cards.warn: Don't edit files directly in the outbox — treat them as drafts that you can review and refine.

cards.title: Quick check
cards.question: What makes this system more efficient than traditional AI prompting?

options.text: Claude interviews you once, then decides and executes multiple tasks autonomously
It works faster than regular Claude

cards.correctFeedback: Right! The efficiency comes from Claude taking initiative after the brief morning interview, eliminating constant back-and-forth prompting.
cards.wrongFeedback: The key is that Claude becomes self-directing — you brief it once, then it chooses and executes multiple tasks on its own.

cards.title: End-of-day summary routine
cards.body: When you're ready to wrap up, trigger Claude's summary routine to see what was accomplished and plan for tomorrow.
cards.codeCaption: Claude will summarize completed work and suggest carry-over items
bullets: Get a clear summary of what was completed
Identify any unfinished tasks that need attention
Receive suggestions for tomorrow's priorities

cards.title: Customize and iterate
cards.body: The system is completely customizable. Edit your claude.md rules file anytime to change how Claude behaves, what it prioritizes, or how it formats deliverables.
bullets: Add new project contexts as your work evolves
Refine communication preferences based on what works
Set boundaries for tasks you want to handle personally
Include examples of output formats you prefer
cards.safe: Start simple and add complexity gradually — you can always refine the rules as you learn what works best for your workflow.

cards.title: Your daily routine checklist
cards.body: Here's your new morning routine with Claude. Bookmark this workflow for consistent daily use.
bullets: Open Claude Co-work in your project folder
Type 'Let's start our day'
Answer the three interview questions honestly
Let Claude work while you focus on high-level tasks
Check outbox periodically for completed deliverables
End with 'end of day' for a summary
cards.warn: Give Claude 10-15 minutes to complete initial tasks before checking for results — good work takes time even with AI.

cards.title: Your AI daily engine is live!
cards.body: You've built a self-directing system that can handle up to 80% of routine daily work. Claude now interviews you each morning, chooses appropriate tasks, and executes them autonomously while you focus on what only you can do.
